---
title: "Smokes"
author: "Harshil Kurapati"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.tutorials)
```

```{r}
smokes |>
  mutate(age_bin = cut(age, breaks = seq(20, 80, by = 5))) |>
  group_by(age_bin, sex) |>
  summarise(pct_smoker = mean(smoke == "Yes"), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = age_bin, y = pct_smoker)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = sex), se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ sex) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +
  labs(
    title = "Share of ever-smokers by age and sex",
    subtitle = "Older adults are more likely to be ever-smokers",
    x = "Age bin",
    y = "Percent ever-smoker",
    caption = "Source: NHANES 2009-2012 via primer.tutorials::smokes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

Smoking remains a major public health concern and the likelihood of being an ever-smoker varies systematically by age and sex. Using a 1,000-adult sample from NHANES 2009-2012 we estimate the difference in probability of being an ever-smoker between a 30-year-old woman and a 70-year-old man.
